articles.title: Mika's Big Tennis Debut for Great Britain
articles.summary: Mika Stojsavljevic is a 17-year-old tennis player from Great Britain who is about to play in her first Billie Jean King Cup match. This is a big team competition where countries compete against each other. Mika won the US Open junior title in 2024, which is a tournament for young players. She will play against Talia Gibson from Australia, who is ranked higher than her. Mika is very excited and said it means everything to her. She got the call from her captain, Anne Keothavong, while she was at the gym. Mika is also studying English Literature and Politics at school while playing tennis. Great Britain is missing some other players, so it will be a tough match. The winner of this tie will go to the finals in Shenzhen in September.

articles.title: Queen Elizabeth II's Famous Clothes in a New Show
articles.summary: Queen Elizabeth II was a very famous queen who wore special clothes that many people recognized. She was queen for 70 years and always wore bright coats and hats when she went out in public. When she was not working, she liked to wear tweed jackets and tartan skirts. A new exhibition at Buckingham Palace shows 200 pieces of her clothing, jewelry, and hats to celebrate her 100th birthday. The exhibition helps us see how Britain changed while she was queen. We picked five of her most iconic outfits. One outfit is a tweed jacket and tartan skirt she wore in the 1950s. It was practical for outdoor activities and made to promote British fashion. Experts say her clothes showed she was in charge without being too loud. Her style influenced modern designers, like a 2024 collection that copied her tartan kilts. Another famous dress is her 1953 Coronation dress, made from silk and decorated with beads and pearls.

articles.title: A Brave Actor's Story
articles.summary: Michael Campbell was an actor from Belfast who had a serious illness called motor neurone disease (MND). He played the role of Richard III in a wheelchair and won a big drama award for his amazing performance. He was 35 years old when he passed away. His wife Naomi and friends said he was very inspiring and full of joy. The Lyric Theatre in Belfast, where he performed, shared how sad they are. Michael showed great strength and made people happy with his acting and storytelling. He will be remembered as a talented and brave person who touched many lives.

articles.title: Tennis Player Gets Angry and Loses Big Match
articles.summary: Daniil Medvedev, a famous tennis player, had a very bad day at a tennis tournament in Monte Carlo. He lost a match 6-0, 6-0, which means he didn't win any games. This was the first time this happened in his career. During the match, he got so upset that he smashed his tennis racquet against the ground until it broke. The crowd watched and found it funny. Medvedev doesn't like playing on clay courts, which is the type of surface in this tournament. He made many mistakes and couldn't win points. His opponent, Matteo Berrettini, played very well and said it was one of his best matches. Berrettini will play another player next. Medvedev is known for getting angry in matches, like at the US Open last year.

articles.title: Euphoria Stars Walk the Red Carpet for New Season
articles.summary: The TV show Euphoria is back with its third season, and the stars came together for a big red carpet event in Los Angeles. This season might be the last one for the show. Zendaya, Sydney Sweeney, and Jacob Elordi are actors who became famous from Euphoria. They play characters like Rue, Cassie, and Nate in the show. The creator, Sam Levinson, said there are no plans for a fourth season right now. Zendaya thinks this could be the end. The new season starts on Sunday, and there are some new actors joining, like Natasha Lyonne. The show is known for its great costumes and filming.

articles.title: BBC Says Sorry for Bad Word on TV
articles.summary: The BBC, a TV company, said they made a mistake during a Bafta awards show. A person with Tourette's shouted a very mean racial word while actors were giving out an award. This bad word was not taken out of the TV broadcast that aired later on BBC One and stayed on iPlayer, a streaming service, until the next morning. Many people complained about this. The BBC's complaints unit said this was wrong and broke their rules, but it was not done on purpose. The team editing the show did not hear the word, so they did not remove it. They did remove another use of the same word later. The BBC said leaving the show on iPlayer for so long made the problem worse. They promised to learn from this and make their processes better to stop it from happening again.
